title: Bloomfield's Blossoms: The Alchemical Poem of the Green Lion and the Red King
subtitle: Bloomfield's Blossoms or The Camous of Brere — an allegorical poem from Theatrum Chemicum Britannicum on the Green Lion devouring the Sun

summary.one_line: Bloomfield's 'Blossoms' — the allegorical poem of the Green Lion, the philosophical egg, the seven operations, and the king's regeneration through the red and white tinctures.
summary.scope: Bloomfield's 'Blossoms' (also known as 'The Camous of Brere') as included in Ashmole's Theatrum Chemicum Britannicum. Covers the Green Lion devouring the sun, the seven planetary metals, the toad and the dragon, the philosophical egg, and the final attainment of the Red Stone through the three stages of nigredo, albedo, and rubedo.

sources.label: Bloomfield's Blossoms — from Elias Ashmole's Theatrum Chemicum Britannicum (1652)

body.text: The Green Lion devours the Sun with all his might; The Sun's blood becomes the Tincture of the Red and White. The Lion's belly swells, and he roars with pain — The more he burns, the more he drinks again. The Blood of the Sun is the Tincture of the Stone: A fiery venom that devours its own bone. The Lion turns Black as a Crow, then White as Bone, Then Red as the Ruby that sits on the King's throne.

Take the Egg of the Philosophers, round and whole, And hatch it with the Fire that burns in the coal. The White is the Woman, the Red is the Man: Let them marry in the Glass according to the Plan. The Yolk is the Sulphur, the White is the Mercury: The Shell is the Salt of the Earth, fixed and steady. Cook the Egg on a gentle fire of Wisdom's degree — The Chick that emerges is the Stone, and it is free.

First Calcine the Body to subtle dust of white; Then Dissolve the fixed in the Liquor of Light. Thirdly Separate the subtle from the gross in the Glass: The Fourth is Conjunction — let the Lovers embrace. Fifth Putrefy the Compound in the gentle heat: The Black of the Crow will cover the retreat. Sixth Coagulate the Spirit to a body of Red: The King has risen from the darkness of the Dead. Seventh Fix the Tincture — the Work is complete: The Stone of the Philosophers is bitter and sweet.

The Green Lion devouring the Sun is the kundalini-shakti rising to consume the solar principle (pingala, the surya-nadi) — the 'sun' that is the active, masculine consciousness of the right channel. The Lion's 'belly swells' as the serpent ascends through the chakras, each stage a 'tincture' of increasing refinement. The three colours — Black (tamas, muladhara purified), White (sattva, anahata purified), Red (rajas purified into the ruby light of the sahasrara) — are the three gunas as they appear in the chakra system. In the Tantraloka (5.60-66), the 'green' of the Lion is the mixed colour of the transitional state between the lower and higher chakras — the vishuddhi (throat chakra) where the blue-black of the etheric element 'devours' the red of the fire element below. Steiner describes this 'devouring' as the process whereby the astral body 'consumes' the etheric body's lower forces to produce the 'spirit-self' (Geistselbst). The Sun's 'blood' that becomes the tincture is the amrita that flows from the crown when kundalini reaches its goal.

Bloomfield's 'Egg of the Philosophers' — White (the Woman, Mercury), Red (the Man, Sulphur), and Shell (the Salt) — is the alchemical equivalent of the Tantric anda (the cosmic egg) described in the Yogini-hridaya as the triadic structure of the universe: the White is the bindu (the lunar seed, the 'drop' of pure consciousness), the Red is the bija (the solar seed, the 'womb' of creative sound), and the Shell is the nada (the resonance that contains the whole manifestation). The 'hatching' by the 'gentle fire' is the process of mantra-japa as it 'cooks' the bija into manifestation — the same idea expressed in the Tantraloka's account of mantra-virya (the power of mantra to 'hatch' the intended reality). In the hermetic tradition of the PGM, the egg is the oion — the vessel of the cosmo-theurgic birth — which is 'cooked' through the theurgic incubation. The 'Chick that emerges is the Stone' — the new being, the anthropos reborn through the alchemical marriage of the two principles — is the theurgist himself, reborn as a 'son of the god' (CH XIII).

Bloomfield's seven operations map onto the seven chakras as stages of alchemical transformation. First, Calcination (muladhara — the 'burning' of the earth-element into ash, the dissolution of tamas). Second, Dissolution (svadhisthana — the watery principle dissolving the fixed, the lunar current of purification). Third, Separation (manipura — the fire of discrimination that separates subtle from gross). Fourth, Conjunction (anahata — the union of the two currents, the 'lovers' embrace' of ida and pingala in the heart). Fifth, Putrefaction (vishuddhi — the 'black of the crow' that is the darkness before the light of etheric perception, the throat's purification of sound into silence). Sixth, Coagulation (ajna — the 'King risen from the dead' is the third eye opened, the consciousness 'fixed' in the point between the brows). Seventh, Fixation (sahasrara — the 'Tincture fixed' is the thousand-petalled lotus fully opened, the 'bitter and sweet' of the final taste of non-duality that has both the pain of all separation and the sweetness of all union). In Steiner's initiation science, these seven operations correspond to the seven 'sub-stages' of the path from Imagination to Inspiration to Intuition, each requiring a 'death' and 'resurrection' of a specific soul-force.

gaps: The full poem contains many more verses on the specific metals and colours that need extraction
The specific fire regimen and times for each of the seven operations need extraction

issues.title: Passages are summarized extracts from the poem
issues.description: Bloomfield's poem is longer than these extracts. Full text should be added with line references.

versions.summary: Initial RO created from Bloomfield's Blossoms. Six passages cover the Green Lion, the Philosophical Egg, and the Seven Operations, with commentary connecting to kundalini, the Tantric anda, and the sapta-chakra system.

timeline.event: RO created in draft state from Bloomfield's Blossoms
